notes: The standalone `ncurses-tinfo-cabi` cdylib is a true minimal `libtinfo.so.6`: it exports exactly the nine low-level terminfo symbols (setupterm, tigetstr/num/flag, tputs, putp, tgoto, tparm, curses_version) and no curses screen symbols, and a C program using only those, dynamically linked against it, produces output byte-identical to the system libtinfo. This is the termlib split bash/less/readline depend on. Closes the libtinfo half of BLD-05 (the `libncursesw` .so / NCURSES.SONAME court already serves the monolithic role).
oracle_method: readelf SONAME + nm -D exported-symbol set of the standalone libncurses_tinfo_cabi.so; then link a tinfo-only C program against it (as libtinfo.so.6 via rpath) vs the system libtinfo and diff stdout
